video.title: One-Pan Lemon Garlic Pasta | The Easiest One-Pot Pasta Recipe

segments.text:
so today we're making a very traditional dish from 
the mediterranean kitchen we're talking a lemon

garlic pasta now the technique we're going to 
use to cook this dish is the one pot method which

means the pasta gets cooked in the same pan as the 
rest of the ingredients that way the starch in the

pasta stays in the dish and let me tell you this 
gives it the most incredible texture & flavor

but the best part this recipe is so easy to make 
it's got so many simple yet beautiful flavors and

it comes together in under 30 minutes truly the 
perfect dish for a busy week night dinner or for

relaxed weekend lunch next to a bottle of spanish 
wine let's begin by grabbing a large fry pan

important to use a larger pan here this 
is a one pot pasta you want to make sure

you have enough room for the pasta and the 
liquid i'm gonna heat it with a medium heat

and add in a generous 2 tablespoons of extra virgin 
olive oil which is 35 milliliters now the olive

oil i'm using to make this pasta dish is 
from apollo this is their miller's favorite

organic extra virgin olive oil it's got 
such a beautiful depth of flavors and it

works fabulous with this pasta dish link where 
you can get some in the description box below

while the olive oil is heating 
i'm gonna grab one head of garlic
remove the skins from the cloves
and roughly chop the garlic into small

chunks after heating the olive oil for three 
minutes on a medium heat it should be nice and hot

but as you can see it's not smoking so it 
hasn't lost any of its beautiful flavor

at this point i'm gonna start adding 
in the chopped garlic into the pan

and start mixing the chunks of 
garlic around with the olive oil

you want to make sure you mix this continuously 
that way all the garlic is evenly sauteed

after about two minutes and 
the garlic is lightly sauteed

you don't want to brown it too much i'm 
gonna grab one teaspoon of dried thyme

which is about one gram and add 
it into the pan with the garlic

and we'll give this a quick mix that way that dry 
thyme is evenly coating all the pieces of garlic

then we'll add in three cups of vegetable broth 
which is 710 milliliters the broth brings out

an incredible flavor in the pasta but if you 
prefer you can just use water with a pinch

of salt you'll find a link on how to make my 
homemade vegetable broth in the iCard above

we'll turn up the fire from 
a medium heat to a high heat

and we'll give it a quick mix 
and bring the broth to a boil

once the broth comes to a boil 
it's time to add in the pasta

i'm going to be using penne pasta here but you can 
use any other type of thick pasta for this recipe

i'm going to measure out two cups which 
is 200 grams this recipe feeds two people

that's one cup per person and i'm going to 
add in the penne pasta into the boiling broth

and we'll give this a quick mix that way
all the ingredients are evenly distributed

and you don't have to mix this continuously 
but do get in there every two to three minutes

that way the pasta is evenly cooked 
and it doesn't stick together

after about 10 minutes and you can see a lot 
of that broth has been absorbed by the

pasta but there's still plenty of broth 
left i'm going to place a lid on the pan

and lower the fire to a low medium heat and 
we're going to simmer this between three to

four minutes on a low medium heat this 
is enough time for the majority of that

broth to be absorbed by the pasta and 
for the pasta to be perfectly cooked al dente

after about four minutes i'm 
gonna remove the lid from the pan

and as you can see there's still a little bit of 
broth left in the pan which is totally normal but

it's really thickened up thanks to the starch from 
the pasta so it's kind of like a little sauce in

there that really elevates the flavors of this 
dish at this point i'm going to remove the pan

from the heat for the final touches to our pasta 
dish i'm going to finely grate in some lemon zest

i'm going to add in the lemon zest of half 
of a lemon and make sure you don't add any

of the white pith in there otherwise it's 
going to add a bitter flavor to your pasta

then we'll season the pasta with a kiss of 
sea salt and some freshly cracked black pepper

finely grated some manchego cheese the 
one that i'm using has been aged for

six months so it's got a nice bite to it 
but you can use whatever cheese you like

and we'll top it off with some 
finely chopped parsley our one pan

lemon garlic pasta is done 
you saw this very easy to make

beautiful presentation done in under 30 minutes 
let's give it a try and see how it tastes
lemon garlic pasta here we go

it is so good but most importantly the pasta is 
perfectly al dente so it's got a nice bite to

it and as for the flavors you have all that 
garlic the fresh lemon zest works so well

together the little kiss of manchego cheese and 
the dried thyme truly an explosion of so much goodness

but the beauty of this dish is that it's a 
one pot pasta so all the starch stayed in

there which gives it the most beautiful rich 
flavor folks you saw this easy to make done

in under 30 minutes really quick before i go a 
shout out to a couple of my patreons Nancy T

David Anthony and Michael Fenter again guys thank you 
so much for being patreons of spain on a fork it's

thanks to you and the rest of my patreons
why spain on a fork continues to move forward

if you're not a patreon consider becoming one 
it really helps with ingredients and equipment

to continue making videos like this you'll find 
my patreon link in the description box below and i card

above if you enjoyed today's video hit that 
like button leave me a comment below and if you're

not subscribed you know what to do smash that 
subscribe button till the next time...hasta luego!!
